title: Aesculus × mutabilis
taxonomy: Aesculus × mutabilis is a hybrid of the Aesculus flava and Aesculus pavia species, classified under the family Sapindaceae. It is a deciduous tree known for its stunning fall colors. As a hybrid, Aesculus × mutabilis does not have a widely recognized species classification, but it is often considered a cultivar. The classification of Aesculus × mutabilis can vary depending on the specific cultivar, but it is generally classified as a member of the Aesculus genus. The hybrid is often cultivated for its attractive foliage and vibrant fall colors.
benefits: Aesculus × mutabilis, also known as the 'Red Horse Chestnut', has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting the body from oxidative stress. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Aesculus × mutabilis in treating various health conditions. Some studies suggest that the plant may also have antiviral and antibacterial properties, making it a potential candidate for further research in the development of new medicines.
properties: Aesculus × mutabilis, also known as the changing or variable buckeye, is not commonly documented to have known medicinal properties. However, its closest relative, Aesculus hippocastanum, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The seeds and leaves of Aesculus hippocastanum have been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and as a diuretic. The plant's aesculin content has been studied for its potential cardiovascular benefits and antioxidant properties. More research is needed to confirm the specific therapeutic properties of Aesculus × mutabilis.
constituents: Aesculus × mutabilis contains active constituents such as isoflavones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits. These compounds have been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The isoflavones present in Aesculus × mutabilis may also have estrogenic effects, which could impact hormone regulation and cardiovascular health. Additionally, the plant's flavonoids may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The specific active constituents and their effects may vary depending on the plant's source, preparation, and individual tolerance.

intro: Aesculus × mutabilis, also known as the Buckeye tree, is a hybrid plant species in the Hippocastanaceae family, characterized by its distinctive palmate leaves and attractive flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may aid in the treatment of fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aesculus × mutabilis include aesculin, aesculetin, and aescoside, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or syrup, and is commonly used to treat various ailments, particularly those related to the respiratory and gastrointestinal systems.
